entries.title: Prompt injection sanitization with structural stripping
entries.description: variableSanitizer.ts implements multi-layer prompt injection prevention: strips section delimiters and role overrides, removes invisible Unicode (zero-width chars, ANSI escapes, non-BMP homoglyphs), and applies type-aware sanitization (URL: HTTPS-only + no private IPs; JSON: parse-reserialize; text: escape markdown headings and backticks). This prevents injected content from breaking out of prompt variable substitution boundaries.

entries.title: URL sanitization blocks SSRF via private IP detection
entries.description: sanitizeUrl.ts validates URLs against private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.0.0.1, localhost), blocks file://, javascript:, data: schemes, and rejects embedded credentials (user:pass@). sanitizeIconUrl() enforces HTTPS-only for external resources. This prevents Server-Side Request Forgery when URLs from user input or AI-generated content are used in fetch calls or image sources.

entries.title: Hybrid RSA+AES encryption for IPC credential transport
entries.description: Credential values are encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a random 12-byte IV, then the AES key itself is wrapped with RSA-2048-OAEP. Session keys are rotated per app launch, preventing replay attacks across sessions. Output format is base64(rsa_encrypted_key).base64(iv||ciphertext). This avoids RSA's plaintext size limit (~190 bytes) while providing session-scoped forward secrecy for sensitive IPC payloads.

entries.title: Sensitive data masking in error messages
entries.description: maskSensitive.ts redacts JSON keys matching /password|secret|token|api_key|auth|credential|private_key/i. sanitizeErrorMessage() strips file paths, IP addresses, internal hostnames (.internal, .local, .corp), emails, and inline secrets from error strings. Used both in frontend error display and Sentry event scrubbing, preventing accidental PII/credential exposure in logs and error reports.

entries.title: Tauri CSP locks down content sources
entries.description: The Tauri config enforces a strict Content Security Policy: default-src 'self', script-src 'self' (no eval/inline), frame-src 'none', object-src 'none', with explicit allowlists for img-src (CDN icons, Google avatars) and connect-src (GitHub raw, Sentry). Dev mode relaxes to allow localhost HMR. This prevents XSS escalation even if HTML injection occurs, since injected scripts won't execute under the CSP.
entries.codePattern:
// tauri.conf.json security.csp
"default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' https://cdn.simpleicons.org https://lh3.googleusercontent.com; connect-src 'self' https://*.ingest.sentry.io; frame-src 'none'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'"

entries.title: Security audit script with secret scanning
entries.description: scripts/security-audit.sh runs cargo audit (Rust CVEs), npm audit (Node CVEs, high+critical), and custom regex scanning for leaked secrets: AWS keys (AKIA...), OpenAI/Stripe keys (sk-...), GitHub PATs (ghp_...), private keys, hardcoded passwords. Also checks crypto patterns: nonce randomness (must use OsRng), PBKDF2 iterations >= 600k, no plaintext credentials outside crypto.rs, no unwrap() in crypto paths. Outputs JUnit XML for CI integration.
entries.codePattern:
# Secret patterns
grep -rn 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}' src/ src-tauri/src/
grep -rn 'sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}' src/ src-tauri/src/
grep -rn 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}' src/ src-tauri/src/
grep -rn '-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----' src/ src-tauri/src/

# Crypto checks
grep -rn 'static.*nonce' src-tauri/src/
grep -c 'iterations.*[0-5][0-9][0-9]_' src-tauri/src/crypto.rs

entries.title: Two-database isolation for app vs user data
entries.description: The app uses separate SQLite databases: personas.db holds internal app state (personas, triggers, executions, credentials) with restricted file permissions (0600 Unix, icacls Windows), while personas_data.db holds user-facing data (knowledge bases, documents, vector chunks) that agents can freely read/write. Separate connection pools ensure a compromised agent query can't touch credential storage or execution history.

entries.title: Atomic credential creation with transactions
entries.description: Credential creation with field-level encryption uses database transactions to ensure atomicity. The credential row and its encrypted field rows are inserted within a single transaction — if any field encryption or insert fails, the entire transaction rolls back, preventing orphaned credential rows without fields. The encrypt → insert → commit pattern ensures no partially-encrypted records exist.

entries.title: Parameterized query builder with LIKE escape
entries.description: The memory search query builder uses numbered placeholders (?1, ?2) and a separate escape_like() function that escapes %, _, and \ before wrapping in LIKE wildcards. Parameters are collected into a Vec and bound at execution time, never interpolated. This prevents both SQL injection and LIKE wildcard injection where user input containing % could match unintended rows.

entries.title: Connection pool pragmas for SQLite performance
entries.description: Every connection acquired from the r2d2 pool runs WAL mode, foreign keys ON, busy_timeout 5000ms, synchronous NORMAL, and cache_size 2000 pages (~2MB) via a CustomizeConnection hook. WAL enables concurrent reads during writes, busy_timeout prevents immediate SQLITE_BUSY errors under contention, and NORMAL sync balances durability with performance. Pool size is 4 connections matching typical desktop workloads.

entries.title: Input validation with control character rejection
entries.description: The persona repository validates names before DB writes: checks for empty strings, enforces max length (1000 chars), and rejects control characters by iterating all chars. This prevents invisible characters from corrupting UI rendering or causing unexpected SQL behavior. Validation happens in Rust before any DB operation, not relying on frontend-only validation.

entries.title: Zustand domain-based slice composition
entries.description: Each Zustand store composes multiple domain slices via StateCreator spread. The agentStore combines 7+ slices (persona, tool, execution, lab, chat, budgets), each defining its own state shape and actions. All slices share a CoreState interface with error and isLoading fields. The persist middleware uses partialize() to selectively hydrate only the selectedPersonaId, not cached data, forcing fresh fetches on startup.

entries.title: Centralized EventBridge for Tauri event coordination
entries.description: eventBridge.ts defines a declarative registry of all Tauri event listeners in one file. A single initAllListeners() call sets up subscriptions that route events to the appropriate stores (auth-state-changed → authStore, healing-event → overviewStore, rotation-completed → vaultStore). Auth events are debounced at 100ms to prevent state thrashing. Custom DOM events bridge Tauri backend events to frontend store actions.

entries.title: Error classification with retry guidance
entries.description: ApiError classifies errors as transient (timeout, network, 502/503/429), permanent (400/401/403/404, validation), or unknown. Each classification carries a retryAfterMs hint. The withRetry() wrapper automatically retries transient errors once after the suggested delay. This prevents hammering failing endpoints while ensuring resilient recovery from network hiccups, without complex retry/backoff logic in every store action.

entries.title: Execution state lock prevents concurrent runs
entries.description: The execution slice guards against concurrent executions by checking isExecuting before spawning. The state is locked (isExecuting=true, output cleared) BEFORE the async Tauri invoke, and unlocked in a finally block. This prevents double-submit race conditions where rapid clicks could spawn overlapping CLI processes, which would corrupt the output stream and confuse the execution sink buffer.

entries.title: Request deduplication prevents concurrent identical fetches
entries.description: deduplicateFetch coalesces concurrent calls to the same endpoint by keying on the command+args. If a fetch for the same key is already in-flight, subsequent callers receive the same Promise instead of spawning duplicate IPC calls. This prevents N simultaneous component mounts from triggering N identical API calls, reducing IPC overhead and preventing redundant store updates.

entries.title: Tauri invoke wrapper with timeout and metrics
entries.description: tauriInvoke.ts wraps all Tauri IPC calls with a 30-second timeout via Promise.race against a rejection timer. Every call is recorded in an IPC metrics ring buffer (command, duration, success/fail). Undefined args are coerced to null for Rust Option<T> deserialization compatibility. Command names are type-checked against an auto-generated union type from Rust, preventing typos in IPC calls.

entries.title: IPC metrics ring buffer for observability
entries.description: ipcMetrics.ts maintains a 500-entry circular ring buffer of all IPC calls with command name, duration, success/fail, and timestamp. Provides per-command statistics (p50/p95/p99 latency, timeout rate, error rate) and global summaries. Queries >=29s are flagged as timeouts. React components can subscribe to metric updates for real-time IPC performance dashboards in dev tools.

entries.title: Batched IPC responses prevent N+1 calls
entries.description: The get_persona_detail Tauri command returns the persona plus its tools, triggers, and automations in a single IPC response, avoiding 4 separate calls. Similarly, execution output uses incremental offset-based fetching. This batching pattern is critical for Tauri apps where each IPC call has ~1-5ms overhead from serialization/deserialization across the Rust/JS boundary.

entries.title: Structured Rust error enum with PII scrubbing
entries.description: AppError is a thiserror-derived enum with 10+ variants (Database, NotFound, Validation, RateLimited, Forbidden, etc.). The Serialize impl sanitizes file paths and internal details before sending to the frontend as {error, kind}. The Sentry integration strips sensitive breadcrumb keys (password, token, secret, api_key), redacts UUIDs to hash prefixes, and replaces full URLs with domain names only.

entries.title: Auto-generated TypeScript bindings from Rust via ts-rs
entries.description: Rust types derive ts-rs macros to auto-generate TypeScript interfaces in src/lib/bindings/. Command names are also auto-generated as a union type (CommandName), ensuring IPC calls are type-checked at compile time. Manual overrides (commandNames.overrides.ts) allow forward-declaring commands before the Rust side is built. This eliminates drift between Rust and TypeScript type definitions.

entries.title: Custom ESLint rules enforce design token usage
entries.description: Three custom ESLint rules enforce design system consistency: no-raw-text-classes warns on raw Tailwind text sizes (text-xs through text-4xl) requiring semantic typo-* classes; no-raw-spacing-classes warns on raw padding/gap classes requiring designTokens.ts constants (CARD_PADDING, SECTION_GAP); enforce-base-modal flags role='dialog' without importing BaseModal. This prevents visual inconsistency from ad-hoc sizing and ensures all modals share focus-trap behavior.

entries.title: Feature-based module structure with barrel exports
entries.description: The src/features/ directory isolates each domain (agents, vault, pipeline, overview, etc.) into self-contained modules with sub-features (sub_tools, sub_design, sub_editor). Each feature has its own components/, hooks/, and index.ts barrel export. Stores live separately in src/stores/ with slices/ subdirectory. lib/ holds shared utilities that features import but never import from features. This prevents circular dependencies and makes feature deletion safe.
entries.codePattern:
// Feature isolation
src/features/
  agents/
    sub_tools/components/ToolSelector.tsx
    sub_design/components/ConversationMessageList.tsx
    sub_editor/components/EditorBody.tsx
    index.ts  // barrel export
  vault/
    sub_databases/components/SqlEditor.tsx
    sub_design/setup/setupMarkdownComponents.tsx
    index.ts

// Dependency direction: features → stores → lib (never reverse)

entries.title: Deferred initialization after React mount
entries.description: App.tsx loads BackgroundServices (eventBridge, knowledge middleware, store initialization ~300KB) as a lazy dynamic import that renders after React has mounted. This ensures the UI shell appears before heavy initialization starts. CommandPalette, OnboardingOverlay, GuidedTour, ExecutionMiniPlayer, and toast containers are also deferred. Combined with Vite's manual chunk splitting, this minimizes time-to-interactive.

entries.title: WebView2 compatibility layer for Object.prototype
entries.description: WebView2 (Windows) marks Object.prototype properties (toString, constructor, valueOf) as non-configurable, crashing when libraries like d3, es-toolkit, or decimal.js try to assign them. A two-tier fix applies: a runtime shim (webview2-compat.js) converts existing properties to configurable getters/setters, and an esbuild source transform rewrites `X.prototype.prop = val` to Object.defineProperty with configurable:true. This is specific to Tauri/WebView2 and prevents production crashes.

entries.title: VirtualizedTableBody with threshold fallback
entries.description: Uses @tanstack/react-virtual for tables with >50 rows (fixed 44px row height, 10-item overscan). Below the threshold, renders normally without virtualization overhead. Absolute positioning with transform:translateY provides smooth scrolling. The dual-mode approach avoids unnecessary complexity for small datasets while preventing DOM bloat on large ones.

entries.title: Bundle budget enforcement in CI
entries.description: scripts/check-bundle-budget.mjs parses Vite build output and enforces per-chunk (500KB default) and total bundle (5000KB default) size limits. Reports the top 15 chunks by size and exits with code 1 on violations. Configurable via CLI flags (--max-chunk-kb, --max-total-kb). Combined with manual chunk splitting in vite.config.ts (react-vendor, ui-vendor, chart-vendor, etc.), this prevents bundle size regressions from sneaking into production.

entries.title: Console stripping in production builds
entries.description: Vite's esbuild config drops console.log, console.debug, and console.info in production builds while preserving console.warn and console.error for Sentry error tracking. This reduces bundle size, prevents accidental data leakage through dev logs, and avoids performance overhead from log string formatting in production. The selective approach keeps error reporting functional.

entries.title: Tauri command mock system for Vitest
entries.description: tauriMock.ts provides mockInvoke(cmd, value), mockInvokeOnce(cmd, value), mockInvokeMap(commands), and mockInvokeError(cmd, error) to mock Tauri IPC calls in Vitest tests. Global setup.ts registers @tauri-apps/api/core and @tauri-apps/api/event as vi.fn() mocks. resetInvokeMocks() clears all mocks between tests. This enables testing Tauri-dependent components in jsdom without a running Rust backend.

entries.title: SQLite test database for integration result tracking
entries.description: Integration tests record results into a SQLite database (via better-sqlite3) with schema tracking: round, test_name, provider, model, status, score, duration_ms, cost_usd, token counts, tool_call_count, tools_used, and validation_details. Methods include recordExecution, getByRound, getByProvider, getProviderComparison, and exportJson. WAL mode and foreign keys enabled. This creates an auditable test history for AI model comparison across providers.

entries.title: Feature parity audit test validates completeness
entries.description: featureParity.test.ts (376 lines) systematically audits that all expected matrix features exist and work. It validates cell state machines, vocabulary mappings, glow colors, and completeness ring calculations. Each parity check is a separate test case with descriptive failure messages explaining what's missing and why it matters. This catches feature regressions that unit tests on individual functions would miss.

entries.title: React Flow alignment guides with viewport transforms
entries.description: AlignmentGuides.tsx computes snap lines based on dragged node edges and centers against all other nodes within a 4px SNAP_TOLERANCE. It correctly applies viewport transforms (zoom + pan) to convert between flow coordinates and screen pixels. The guide lines render as absolute-positioned SVG overlays that fade when dragging stops. This provides Figma-like alignment behavior in the workflow canvas.

entries.title: DOMPurify sanitization for highlight.js output
entries.description: sanitizeHtml.ts provides two sanitization modes: sanitizeHljsHtml() allows only <span> with class attributes (for syntax highlighting), while stripHtml() removes ALL tags for AI-generated text. This defense-in-depth approach combines React's automatic text escaping with explicit HTML stripping, preventing XSS even when rendering markdown code blocks with highlight.js which produces raw HTML strings.

entries.title: Markdown URL sanitization blocks javascript: and data: schemes
entries.description: Both markdown renderers (MarkdownRenderer and setupMarkdownComponents) override the <a> tag to run sanitizeExternalUrl() before rendering. This blocks javascript:, data:, and file: URLs while allowing only http/https. Links open in new tabs with target='_blank'. If sanitization fails, the link text is rendered as a plain <span> instead of a clickable link. This prevents XSS via markdown-injected links.

entries.title: Chart error boundary prevents cascade failures
entries.description: ChartErrorBoundary is a class component that wraps Recharts visualizations, catching render errors from malformed data without crashing the entire dashboard. On error it shows a retry button. This is particularly important because Recharts can throw on NaN/Infinity values or empty datasets. Without this boundary, a single bad data point would blank the entire analytics page.

entries.title: Giant components exceed 580 lines without extraction
entries.description: DimensionEditPanel.tsx (584 lines) contains SimpleListEditor, ConnectorCards, ConnectorSwapModal, TriggerCards, and ReviewToggle all in a single file. BundleImportDialog is similarly oversized. While internally organized with sub-functions, these components are hard to test in isolation, create large diffs on any change, and resist code review. Extracting each logical section into its own file would improve testability and reduce cognitive load.

entries.title: State setter racing in async unmount scenarios
entries.description: BundleImportDialog calls setPhase('previewing') then awaits an async operation, followed by another setState without checking if the component is still mounted. If the user closes the dialog during the async gap, React will warn about state updates on unmounted components. The fix is to use a useRef(false) isMounted flag checked before each setState, or better, use an AbortController that cancels on cleanup.

entries.title: Multi-tier builds from single codebase
entries.description: The app supports three product tiers (starter, team, builder) via VITE_APP_TIER environment variable set during build. Separate build scripts (build:starter, build:team, build:builder) produce different bundles from the same source. Feature gates check the tier at runtime to show/hide premium features. This avoids maintaining separate branches or repos for different product SKUs.
